title: afzelia cochinchinensis
intro: Afzelia cochinchinensis, also known as the Southeast Asian tree or keo, is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in Southeast Asian and Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Afzelia cochinchinensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and seeds of the tree are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and other medicinal products.
